stories.headline: Hill Faces 'Brutal' Week: FISA 702 Expires Thursday, DHS Funding Runs Dry April 30, Budget Resolution Due Wednesday
stories.summary: Speaker Mike Johnson is racing to pass a revised FISA Section 702 reauthorization, the Senate-passed budget DHS resolution, and a farm bill this week as DHS hits day 72 of shutdown and Sec. Markwayne Mullin warns pay stops April 30.

stories.headline: WHCD Gunman Cole Allen Faces Federal Court Today; Wiles to Review Secret Service Posture
stories.summary: Suspected White House Correspondents' Dinner shooter Cole Tomas Allen, 31, of Torrance, Calif., appears in federal court today on two gun charges; WH Chief of Staff Susie Wiles will meet Secret Service and DHS leadership this week to review presidential event protocols.

stories.headline: King Charles III Arrives for State Visit; Joint-Session Address Tuesday
stories.summary: King Charles III and Queen Camilla begin a four-day U.S. state visit today with tea at the White House, with the king set to address a joint meeting of Congress on Tuesday — the first British monarch to do so since Queen Elizabeth II in 1991.

stories.headline: Senate Banking Wednesday Vote on Warsh as Fed Chair; Powell's Final FOMC Presser Same Day
stories.summary: The Senate Banking Committee will vote Wednesday on Kevin Warsh's nomination to succeed Jerome Powell as Federal Reserve chair; Powell holds his final FOMC press conference at 2:30 p.m. Wednesday.

stories.headline: Middlesex Township Residents Confront 'Shotgun Marriage' as Central PA's First Hyperscale Data Center Begins Site Work
stories.summary: Site work at central Pennsylvania's first hyperscale data center in Middlesex Township has neighbors complaining of trucks and blasting, the first concrete community-impact dispute as Pennsylvania's data center buildout moves from siting to construction.

stories.headline: Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick Endorses Shapiro for Reelection — and a 2028 Presidential Run
stories.summary: Bucks County Republican Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick told the Inquirer Gov. Josh Shapiro will win reelection and should run for president in 2028, a notable cross-party endorsement from a swing-district incumbent.

stories.headline: PA Human Relations Commission Drops Below Quorum as Shapiro Holds Off on Appointments
stories.summary: The 70-year-old Pennsylvania Human Relations Commission has fallen from seven to five commissioners — below the six required for a quorum — and its executive director plans to leave in June, with the Shapiro administration declining to say when new appointments will come.

stories.headline: Republican State Leadership Committee Adds PA to 2026 Legislative Target List
stories.summary: The RSLC told Broad + Liberty it believes Republicans can flip the PA House and hold their state Senate majority in 2026 even with Shapiro atop the Democratic ticket — signaling outside money inbound for state legislative races.

stories.headline: PJM Market Monitor Asks FERC to Reject Hull Street's 1.3 GW Gas Plant Buy from Rockland Capital
stories.summary: Monitoring Analytics urged FERC on Apr. 24 to reject Hull Street Energy's purchase of the 677-MW Lee County (Ill.) and 590-MW Tait (Ohio) gas plants from Rockland Capital, warning the new owner could redirect the plants from PJM's capacity market to data center loads and shift costs to other customers.

stories.headline: Interior Sec. Burgum Testifies Wednesday Before Senate Energy and Natural Resources
stories.summary: Interior Secretary Doug Burgum is scheduled to appear before Senate ENR at 9:30 a.m. Wednesday — likely to face questioning on public-lands leasing, Susquehanna/Talen-style colocation precedent, and DOI permitting timelines.

stories.headline: EPA Administrator Zeldin Testifies Wednesday Before Senate Environment and Public Works
stories.summary: EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin will appear before Senate EPW at 2:30 p.m. Wednesday — set against this week's separate suit by cities arguing EPA failed to uphold the soot (PM2.5) standard.

stories.headline: House GOP Files Bill to Extend Renewables Tax Credits; NextEra Contracts 4 GW of New Generation in Q1
stories.summary: House Republicans introduced legislation to extend clean-energy tax credits — a notable break with the Trump-era IRA repeal push — as NextEra reported 4 GW of new generation contracted in Q1 and Cities sued EPA over the soot standard.

stories.headline: Steelers Wrap 2026 Draft: Allar in Round 3, Iheanachor Round 1, Bernard Round 2 — McCarthy's First Class Targets QB, OL, WR
stories.summary: Pittsburgh closed the 2026 NFL Draft with Penn State QB Drew Allar in Round 3, Arizona State OL Max Iheanachor at the top of Round 1, Alabama WR Germie Bernard in Round 2 (after a trade up with Indianapolis), and Notre Dame DT Gabriel Rubio in Round 6 — head coach Mike McCarthy's first class as Pittsburgh awaits Aaron Rodgers' decision.

stories.headline: Penguins Stay Alive 4-2 in Game 4 vs. Flyers — Crosby and Rakell Each Goal-and-Assist; Game 5 Wednesday in Philly
stories.summary: Sidney Crosby and Rickard Rakell each had a goal and an assist (Letang scored, Dewar added an empty-netter) as the Penguins beat the Flyers 4-2 in Game 4 of the East First Round to cut the series deficit to 3-1; Game 5 is Wednesday in Philadelphia.

stories.headline: Pirates Blanked 5-0 in Milwaukee as Kyle Harrison K's 12 — Bucs Strike Out 18, Drop Series Finale
stories.summary: Kyle Harrison struck out 12 over six scoreless innings as Milwaukee beat Pittsburgh 5-0 to avoid the sweep; Carmen Mlodzinski (1-2) gave up all five runs in the fourth, and the Pirates (16-12) struck out 18 times before opening a series tonight at home vs. St. Louis.
